body_txt: When a car is wrecked (or explodes), often a lone wheel will be seen rolling (or flying) away from the vehicle. Since Every Car Is a Pinto , it may well be on fire . Expect to also see one of these after an Offscreen Crash , rolling into view from Behind the Black and gradually wobbling to a halt. Essentially, this is the vehicular equivalent of Smoldering Shoes . A more minor version may involve a hubcap or two coming loose during chase scenes . This is most frequently Played for Laughs , though serious examples do exist. Amazingly, this is not an unrealistic or even uncommon phenomenon . Tires really can and do come loose in vehicle accidents, though what fiction only rarely tells you is that they can be quite dangerous — they're heavier than they look, especially truck wheels, and can go a long way without losing much momentum. These things have outright killed people on occasion , and have been known to be a cause of traffic accidents as well . Not to be confused with Freewheelers , which was a 70s British kids' drama series.
